2024-08-01.title: Ex-Green Beret Who Planned Failed 2020 Venezuela Coup Is Arrested by U.S.
2024-08-01.abstract: Jordan Goudreau, 48, had taken credit for a failed coup attempt against Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela. He faces federal charges of illegal arms smuggling.

2024-08-01.title: Track Keeps Banning Drug Cheats. It Worries That Other Sports Are Not.
2024-08-01.abstract: Track and field’s antidoping arm, the Athletics Integrity Unit, has announced a string of cases in recent months. Is that the sign of a problem, or proof that its approach is working?

2024-08-01.title: In an Italian Village, a Mayor Walks Off the Wine and Everyone Joins In
2024-08-01.abstract: Luciano Fregonese, the mayor of a town in Italy known for Prosecco, started walking to counter the calories he consumed at social obligations. His jaunts have become a sensation.

2024-08-01.title: Can Gouda’s Cheesemakers Stall a Sinking Future?
2024-08-01.abstract: The small city where the renowned Dutch cheese is traded is subsiding as sea levels rise. Experts say the industry may not survive there, even with the ingenuity of the country’s water managers.

2024-08-01.title: For Simone Biles, a Long Road Back Is Paved in Gold
2024-08-01.abstract: After winning the all-around gymnastics title, she wore a sparkly necklace in the shape of a goat, for Greatest of All Time. But she is not done yet: She has three more chances to win medals in Paris.

2024-08-02.title: U.S. Recognizes Maduro’s Rival as Winner of Venezuelan Election
2024-08-02.abstract: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken said there was “overwhelming evidence” that Edmundo González had won, despite President Nicolás Maduro’s claim of victory.

2024-08-02.title: Talking Trump and Economics With Joseph Stiglitz in Sydney
2024-08-02.abstract: The eminent economist describes Donald Trump and the MAGA movement as a response to the failure of nearly 40 years of neoliberalism.

2024-08-02.title: Talking Trump and Economics With Joseph Stiglitz in Sydney
2024-08-02.abstract: His new book is about reimagining freedom for ‘progressive capitalism.’

2024-08-02.title: Many of Gaza’s Medical Workers Have Been Detained or Killed
2024-08-02.abstract: Out of a prewar total of about 20,000 health workers, 500 have been killed in the war, according to the W.H.O., and more than 300 are in Israeli detention, Gaza’s health ministry says.

2024-08-02.title: Kids at the Olympics: ‘It’s Honestly Kind of Insane.’
2024-08-02.abstract: From the shooting range to the swimming pool, teens and preteens are grabbing medals and the spotlight. Nowhere is this truer than at the skateboard park.

2024-08-03.title: To Lam Confirmed as Vietnam’s Top Leader Until at Least 2026
2024-08-03.abstract: Mr. Lam had been filling the post of general secretary temporarily since the death of his predecessor in July. Now he is in position to consolidate power.

2024-08-03.title: Iran Arrests Dozens in Search for Suspects in Killing of Hamas Leader
2024-08-03.abstract: Iran has begun a sweeping investigation into the assassination of Ismail Haniyeh, with its intensity a sign of how damaging and shocking the security failure was.

2024-08-03.title: Marcell Jacobs Has Waited a Long Time to Prove the Doubters Wrong
2024-08-03.abstract: When the Italian sprinter came from nowhere to win 100-meter gold in Tokyo, whispers spread about his sudden rise. In Paris, he hopes to silence them for good.

2024-08-03.title: Bombs Rain Down in Myanmar as Junta Evades Sanctions to Buy Jet Fuel
2024-08-03.abstract: The military has stepped up a campaign of airstrikes, and killed more civilians, to fend off rebel advances.

2024-08-03.title: Equestrian Is Having an Uncomfortable Moment. Its Olympic Horses Are Not.
2024-08-03.abstract: Accusations of mistreatment cast a shadow over dressage and other events. Can cooling tents and locally sourced hay change the narrative?

2024-08-04.title: Meet the Nations That Have Never Won an Olympic Medal (and Two That Just Did)
2024-08-04.abstract: Gold medals in track and field brought St. Lucia and Dominica into an exclusive club of nations. But for 66 other countries and territories, the wait for an Olympic medal goes on, and on, and on.

2024-08-04.title: War Shatters Dating Scene for Women in Ukraine
2024-08-04.abstract: While the pursuit of love might seem secondary to dealing with the horrors and privations of the war, many Ukrainians say they need romantic relationships to help them cope.

2024-08-04.title: The Olympic Flame Melts Parisian Cool
2024-08-04.abstract: There are people walking around the world’s fashion capital with fluffy cockerels on their heads. This is not normal.

2024-08-04.title: Hezbollah and Israel Exchange Limited Fire Over the Border
2024-08-04.abstract: The attack from Lebanon did not appear to be the major retaliation that Hezbollah has threatened in the wake of the Israeli assassination of a senior commander.

2024-08-04.title: Chinese Swimming’s Olympic Haul: More Medals, More Questions
2024-08-04.abstract: A meet overshadowed by questions about doping produced more medals, but fewer golds, than China’s swimmers won at the Tokyo Games.

2024-08-05.title: Japan Needs Foreign Workers. It’s Just Not Sure It Wants Them to Stay.
2024-08-05.abstract: Foreign employees have become much more visible in Japan. But policies designed only for short-term stays may hurt the country in the global competition for labor.

2024-08-05.title: How Norway Became a Powerhouse for All Seasons
2024-08-05.abstract: With money from an oil boom, Norway, a force at the Winter Olympics for generations, is now churning out elite performers in track, soccer and other sports, too.

2024-08-05.title: As Unrest in Bangladesh Intensifies, All Eyes Are on the Army
2024-08-05.abstract: The military has a history of coups and counter coups. But in this showdown, it has seemed uncomfortable with the police crackdown on protesters.

2024-08-05.title: Flavor Flav Wants You to Watch More Water Polo
2024-08-05.abstract: The rapper has become a self-appointed hype man and benefactor to the U.S. women’s team in Paris. He plans to keep the poolside party going into 2028.

2024-08-05.title: Britain’s Violent Riots: What We Know
2024-08-05.abstract: Officials had braced for more unrest on Wednesday, but the night’s anti-immigration protests were smaller, with counterprotesters dominating the streets instead.

2024-08-06.title: Long Battle for a Ruined City Takes a Desperate Turn
2024-08-06.abstract: Ukrainian troops are engaged in a harsh struggle for Toretsk, a strategic city in eastern Ukraine that has been under relentless assault by Russian forces.

2024-08-06.title: To Save His Shrinking City, a Mayor Turns to Koreans Uprooted by Stalin
2024-08-06.abstract: Like many South Korean cities, Jecheon is being eroded by rapid aging and rock-bottom birthrates. Can ethnic Korean migrants from Central Asia turn it around?

2024-08-06.title: As Ukraine Collects Medals in Paris, Its Sports Pipeline Is in Tatters
2024-08-06.abstract: Facilities have been battered. Coaches are in short supply. Children have moved abroad. Ukraine faces a long hard road toward rebuilding its athletic programs.

2024-08-06.title: One of China’s Most Talkative Nationalists Suddenly Goes Silent
2024-08-06.abstract: Hu Xijin’s tens of millions of followers have heard nothing from him since late July. Some think one of his posts praising Beijing may have backfired.

2024-08-06.title: One Olympian’s Secret Weapon? His High School Coach.
2024-08-06.abstract: All elite athletes make changes to their lives to keep their careers moving forward. For the American distance runner Grant Fisher, the key to success was in his past.

2024-08-07.title: Can You Heal Olympic Heartbreak With Belated Medals?
2024-08-07.abstract: A weight lifter walked away from his sport after a bitter loss in 2012. He and other athletes, including a U.S. figure skating team, will receive their medals in Paris.

2024-08-07.title: How Bangladesh’s Leader Lost Her Grip on Power
2024-08-07.abstract: Once viewed as the hope for a democratic future in Bangladesh, Sheikh Hasina turned increasingly autocratic during her years as prime minister. Weeks of violent protests ultimately forced her from office on Monday. Mujib Mashal, South Asia bureau chief for The New York Times, explains how Ms. Hasina fought until the very end.

2024-08-07.title: With ‘Sprint’ and Simone Biles, Netflix Fills Olympic Content Gap
2024-08-07.abstract: New seasons of documentaries about running, gymnastics and basketball are being filmed this summer as part of a partnership with the International Olympic Committee.

2024-08-07.title: Disqualification Costs India a Gold Medal, but Its Future Still Looks Bright
2024-08-07.abstract: The wrestler Vinesh Phogat had a chance to become the first woman from India to win an Olympic gold, but she was disqualified when she missed her weight class by a few ounces.

2024-08-07.title: After French Rail Sabotage, Some See Signs of a Murky ‘Ultraleft’
2024-08-07.abstract: Experts say the arson attacks before the opening of the Paris Olympics bear the hallmarks of insurrectionary anarchists opposed to the state.

2024-08-08.title: Trying to Capitalize on Anti-Migrant Riots Could Backfire on U.K.’s Far Right
2024-08-08.abstract: The public’s reaction to the recent rioting has been resoundingly negative, suggesting that using the unrest to push anti-immigrant policies could backfire, at least in the short term.

2024-08-08.title: No Hands, Please: We’re Dutch
2024-08-08.abstract: After two pandemic-disrupted Olympics, most teams haven’t given Covid a second thought in Paris. The one from the Netherlands is the exception.

2024-08-08.title: Bangladesh Swears In Nobel Laureate Yunus After Days of Chaos
2024-08-08.abstract: Muhammad Yunus, as interim leader, faces the daunting task of restoring order in a country gripped by violence and uncertainty.

2024-08-08.title: Defying Spanish Arrest Warrant, Carles Puigdemont Returns to Catalonia
2024-08-08.abstract: Large crowds greeted the former regional leader, who has lived in self-imposed exile since a failed 2017 independence bid. Despite a new amnesty law, he could still face arrest.

2024-08-08.title: Japan Warns of Elevated Risk of Giant Earthquake After Strong One Hits
2024-08-08.abstract: The official warning came after a 7.1-magnitude temblor struck off the coast of southern Japan on Thursday.

2024-08-09.title: Liverpool Sends a Message to Far-Right Rioters: Not Here
2024-08-09.abstract: The city in northern England has a long history of protest. That tradition continued this week, with counterprotesters delivering a firm rejection of anti-immigrant violence.

2024-08-09.title: A Pastry That Tastes Like Home
2024-08-09.abstract: The cheese scone is so essential to the New Zealand diet that it is almost impossible to find a cafe without a plate of them on the counter.

2024-08-09.title: The Olympic Marathon Course Is a Torture Test. That’s by Design.
2024-08-09.abstract: “Oh, my gosh,” one Olympian gasped when she got her first look at the hilly, demanding course. “This looks ridiculous.”

2024-08-09.title: A Show of Support for Hamas in a Rival’s Backyard
2024-08-09.abstract: After Hamas’s political leader was killed, some honored him in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, where a rival Palestinian faction is dominant.

2024-08-09.title: Meet Rebeca Andrade, Brazil’s Powerhouse Gymnast
2024-08-09.abstract: Rebeca Andrade of Brazil, who finished second to Simone Biles in the all-around and vault finals, won gold in the floor routine. She talked to us about her experience in Paris.

2024-08-10.title: Israel Launches Another Offensive in Gaza’s South Amid Push for Truce
2024-08-10.abstract: The United States, Egypt and Qatar are trying to restart peace talks between Hamas and Israel, while Israel carries on its operation in Gaza and braces for attacks by Iran and Hezbollah.

2024-08-10.title: China’s Great Wall of Villages
2024-08-10.abstract: China has moved thousands of people to new settlements on its frontiers. It calls them “border guardians.”

2024-08-10.title: Bangladesh Put Its Eggs in One Economic Basket. Now, a Reckoning.
2024-08-10.abstract: Garment exports lifted the country to new heights. But overreliance on that industry also helped bring down Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.

2024-08-10.title: Dear Pentathletes: Enjoy Your Last Ride. Get Ready for the Obstacle Course.
2024-08-10.abstract: Pentathlon will trade show jumping for an obstacle course in 2028, after accusations of horse mistreatment in Tokyo. Athletes aren’t thrilled. But they could have been pillow fighting.

2024-08-10.title: What We Know About the Plane Crash in Brazil
2024-08-10.abstract: The twin-engine turboprop passenger aircraft crashed near São Paulo, killing all 62 people onboard. Officials are investigating the cause.

2024-08-11.title: The Olympics, a Triumph of Ambition, Lift France From Its Gloom
2024-08-11.abstract: All sectors of society came together to achieve the nation’s dream for the Games. Success has shown that unity is possible, if often elusive.

2024-08-11.title: Behind the Scenes of the ‘Treasure Hunt’ for Olympic History
2024-08-11.abstract: Members of the Olympic Museum’s heritage acquisitions team fan out during the Games to get donations to put on display in Lausanne, Switzerland.

2024-08-11.title: The Summer Camps of Ukraine’s Forests: Hikes, First Aid and Military Readiness
2024-08-11.abstract: As the once carefree summers of Ukrainian childhood and young adulthood are forever altered by war, traditional camps have taken on a more patriotic tone.

2024-08-11.title: Rethinking Tourism With the Renewal of a Beloved Italian Path
2024-08-11.abstract: Officials hope the reopening of the Via dell’Amore will be an opportunity to rethink tourism in Cinque Terre, getting visitors off the beaten path.

2024-08-11.title: Sidestepping Deployed Kenyan Forces, Haiti Gangs Continue Reign of Terror
2024-08-11.abstract: Weeks after an international police force began arriving to take on gangs, armed groups are redirecting their campaign of terror outside the capital.

2024-08-12.title: The Filipinos Living in the Shadow of China’s Military Might
2024-08-12.abstract: More than 200 civilian settlers on a contested island in the South China Sea find themselves on the frontier of a possible conflict with China.

2024-08-12.title: Firefighters Battle ‘Extremely Dangerous’ Wildfire Near Athens
2024-08-12.abstract: The blaze broke out in a town near the Greek capital on Sunday, damaging buildings and prompting evacuations. Strong winds have helped the fire spread.

2024-08-12.title: U.S. Moves a Guided-Missile Submarine to the Middle East Amid Fears of Escalation
2024-08-12.abstract: Defense Secretary Lloyd J. Austin III spoke with his Israeli counterpart, Yoav Gallant, the Pentagon said. In an unusual disclosure, it said Mr. Austin had ordered a submarine to the Middle East.

2024-08-12.title: A Nazi Villa So Tainted Berlin Can’t Give It Away
2024-08-12.abstract: The former estate of Joseph Goebbels, the Nazi propaganda minister, is too expensive to keep and perilous to sell. No one knows quite what to do with it.

2024-08-12.title: Britain’s Anti-Immigrant Riots Pose Critical Test for Starmer
2024-08-12.abstract: Even after restoring order, the new prime minister faces a bigger challenge: defusing the issues of fraying public services and a cost of living crisis that underlie the unrest.

2024-08-13.title: Where Students Run the Streets: Bangladesh in Limbo
2024-08-13.abstract: The young protesters who felled an autocrat are now cabinet ministers and traffic cops, trying to restore order and chart a new future for a nation of 170 million.

2024-08-13.title: Why China’s and Russia’s Militaries Are Training Together
2024-08-13.abstract: The two countries have held joint exercises near Alaska, Taiwan and Japan in defiance of the United States and its alliances.

2024-08-13.title: Israel Was Less Flexible in Recent Gaza Cease-Fire Talks, Documents Show
2024-08-13.abstract: The country made five new demands, according to documents detailing its newest negotiating positions.

2024-08-13.title: On the 75th anniversary of the Geneva Conventions, the war in Gaza resonates.
2024-08-13.abstract: Rules that were adopted to guide conduct in conflicts after World War II are as relevant as ever 75 years after the ratification of these directives, known as the Geneva Conventions. But debate rages about the global community’s commitment to them.

2024-08-13.title: Fires Near Athens Leave a Path of Destruction
2024-08-13.abstract: The Greek authorities said the threat posed by the blazes had lessened as of Tuesday, thanks in part to an overnight lull in the strong winds. But the risk remained.

2024-08-14.title: Japan’s Leader, Fumio Kishida, Will Step Down
2024-08-14.abstract: The unpopular prime minister is bowing to pressure within his party. But it is unclear whether the party is truly ready to choose a candidate who will meet the public’s concerns.

2024-08-14.title: He Fled to the U.K. for Safety. Then an Anti-Immigrant Mob Attacked.
2024-08-14.abstract: Abdulmoiz, an asylum seeker from Sudan, feared for his life when far-right rioters tried to set fire to the hotel where he was housed. The violence has eased, but he is still afraid.

2024-08-14.title: Our Reporter on the Reshaping of the Panama Canal
2024-08-14.abstract: After a drought that hampered shipping, the Panama Canal’s overseers are eager to expand its water storage. Climate change leaves them no choice.

2024-08-14.title: China’s Extreme Fan Culture Makes Olympic Gold a Mixed Blessing
2024-08-14.abstract: Fans have mobbed athletes in public and staked out their homes. State media outlets denounced their “visibly aggressive” behavior.

2024-08-14.title: New Zealand Charity Accidentally Gives Away Meth Disguised as Candy
2024-08-14.abstract: Police were racing to recover the fake sweets, each of which contained up to 300 doses of methamphetamine. Three people sought medical attention.

2024-08-15.title: What to Know About the State of the Cease-Fire Talks
2024-08-15.abstract: As tensions in the Middle East escalate, Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken said after meeting with the Israeli prime minister on Monday that Israel had accepting a “bridging proposal.”

2024-08-15.title: Ukraine’s Incursion Into Russia Flips the Script on Putin
2024-08-15.abstract: The reality of 130,000 displaced Russians and a chaotic official response may begin to puncture the official line that Russia is steadily heading toward victory.

2024-08-15.title: Who’s Up Next in Japan? Here Are 5 Potential Leaders.
2024-08-15.abstract: Japan’s Liberal Democratic Party is searching for a successor to Prime Minister Fumio Kishida. But is it willing to change to appease a dissatisfied public?

2024-08-15.title: Typhoon Ampil Approaches Japan, Disrupting Flights and Trains
2024-08-15.abstract: The powerful storm is expected to bring heavy rain, strong winds and the threat of floods and landslides to eastern Japan, including Tokyo, on Friday.

2024-08-15.title: 37 Square Feet That Show Brazil’s Racist Past
2024-08-15.abstract: In Brazil, rooms for maids, a vestige of the country’s history of slavery, are disappearing or being transformed as the country confronts deeply ingrained inequities.

2024-08-16.title: Bangladeshi Students Took Down the Government. Then They Took Over.
2024-08-16.abstract: Just weeks ago, young protesters who opposed the prime minister were clashing with the police. Now, in effect, they are the police.

2024-08-16.title: A Rescue Dog Went Blind. Now She’s Helping Other Pups Avoid Her Fate.
2024-08-16.abstract: Shola, who assisted with search-and-rescue operations in the U.K., has moved to her second act: saving other English Shepherds from blindness.

2024-08-16.title: At 19, a Hong Kong Panda Is Finally a Mother
2024-08-16.abstract: Ying Ying is the oldest panda on record to give birth for the first time. Also unusual: her cubs were naturally conceived, a rarity for the vulnerable species.

2024-08-16.title: Ski Passes and Weber Grills? Obscure Perks of a U.S. Alliance
2024-08-16.abstract: The military alliance between Australia and the United States is growing ever close r. But how does the superpower value its smaller ally? Here are some clues.

2024-08-16.title: Typhoon Ampil Lashes Eastern Japan, Forcing Evacuations
2024-08-16.abstract: The authorities have warned that the storm, which has already flooded some streets in the Tokyo area, could bring violent winds and cause landslides.

2024-08-17.title: Doubting America’s ‘Nuclear Umbrella,’ Some South Koreans Want Their Own
2024-08-17.abstract: Washington says it would defend the South against North Korea with nuclear arms, if need be. But more and more people in the South think it should rely on itself.

2024-08-17.title: Israeli Strike on Lebanon Kills at Least 10
2024-08-17.abstract: The strike came as tensions between the two countries were running high over the Gaza war. Negotiators are pushing for a truce in Gaza, hoping to avert a wider regional conflagration.

2024-08-17.title: ‘There Is No Childhood in Gaza’
2024-08-17.abstract: A 9-year-old Palestinian boy lost his mother, father and two siblings in an Israeli airstrike early in the Gaza war. Within months, he, too, was killed.

2024-08-17.title: With Purple Gold and Bouncy Metal, a Canadian Chemist Shines on YouTube
2024-08-17.abstract: Disillusioned with grad school, Nigel Braun dropped out to film chemistry videos in his parents’ garage in Montreal. Then millions began viewing his whimsical and occasionally dangerous experiments.

2024-08-17.title: Ukraine Destroys Key Russian Bridge as It Presses On With Offensive
2024-08-17.abstract: The bridge’s destruction is a setback for Russian supply lines as Ukraine seeks to consolidate its territorial gains in the Kursk region of western Russia.

2024-08-18.title: A Family Flees and a Mother Mourns After Israeli Settlers Attack a Palestinian Village
2024-08-18.abstract: Residents of the village in the Israeli-occupied West Bank say one Palestinian man was killed by rampaging settlers. Israel’s military confirmed the village was attacked.

2024-08-18.title: Has Power Moderated Italy’s Leader? Not to Same-Sex Parents.
2024-08-18.abstract: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has mostly shown a pragmatic streak abroad. But at home, her government is plunging many gay families into panic.

2024-08-18.title: Venezuelans Mourn Loved Ones Killed in Protests, and Last Shreds of Democracy
2024-08-18.abstract: The nation is in anguish as it buries its dead and enters a new era of authoritarianism.

2024-08-18.title: ‘Bad Blood’ Stalks a Lithium Mine in Serbia
2024-08-18.abstract: A Rio Tinto mine that Europe sees as a critical source for electric vehicle batteries has been the target of enormous protests. “I don’t need green cars. I need green apples and green grass,” said one opponent.

2024-08-18.title: A City of Blaring Sirens Fills With Russians Who Fled Ukraine’s Advance
2024-08-18.abstract: In the regional capital, those who left their homes as Ukrainian forces tore into western Russia are uncertain whether they can ever return to their old lives.

2024-08-19.title: Sparing the Horse, Brussels Warms Up to the Electric Carriage
2024-08-19.abstract: The city’s last horse-drawn carriage operator says business is good since making the switch, placing the Belgian capital in the vanguard of a global change.

2024-08-19.title: A Deadly Fire Exposes the Plight of Low-Paid Migrants in Wealthy Kuwait
2024-08-19.abstract: After the blaze killed 49 migrant workers, a government crackdown focused on building violations — bypassing deeper problems that leave migrants with low wages and unsafe housing across the oil-rich Gulf.

2024-08-19.title: The Mennonites Making the Amazon Their Home
2024-08-19.abstract: Groups of Mennonites, seeking inexpensive land far from modern life, are carving out new colonies in the Amazon. They are also raising fears that they are adding to the deforestation of the vital jungle.

2024-08-19.title: Survivors of Doomsday Starvation Cult Testify Against Pastor and 93 Associates
2024-08-19.abstract: An evangelical pastor in Kenya ordered his flock to shun education and medicine and starve their children to death in order to meet Jesus, witnesses in a manslaughter trial said.

2024-08-19.title: Ukraine Says Its Incursion Will Bring Peace. Putin’s Plans May Differ.
2024-08-19.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky wants to hold Russian territory as leverage in future talks. In Moscow, many doubt the strategy.

2024-08-20.title: Mexico’s Judges Vote to Strike, Opposing Overhaul of Legal System
2024-08-20.abstract: President Andrés Manuel López Obrador wants judges elected, not appointed. Court workers have already walked out to protest his plan, which critics call a power grab.

2024-08-20.title: They Ran a Campaign in Hiding. Now They Wait.
2024-08-20.abstract: The campaign officials for Venezuela’s opposition have sheltered in an Argentine diplomatic residence in Caracas for five months, watching the country’s turmoil from asylum.

2024-08-20.title: Search Resumes for British Mogul and 5 Others After Yacht Sinks Off Sicily
2024-08-20.abstract: The body of the vessel’s cook was recovered while divers searched the hull of the Bayesian for passengers, including the tech entrepreneur Mike Lynch.

2024-08-20.title: Israel Says It Recovered Bodies of 6 Hostages in Gaza
2024-08-20.abstract: The bodies were retrieved in an overnight operation in southern Gaza, the Israeli military said. Five of the hostages were already known to have died in captivity.

2024-08-20.title: Blinken Pushes for Gaza Cease-Fire With Visits to Egypt and Qatar
2024-08-20.abstract: After meetings in Israel, Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken was pressing Hamas’s leadership through intermediaries to continue talks on the deal.

2024-08-21.title: China’s ‘Road Trip Auntie’ Is Ready for a New Milestone: Divorce
2024-08-21.abstract: Su Min became an internet sensation for leaving behind an abusive husband to drive across China alone. Now she’s ending the marriage, but there will be a price.

2024-08-21.title: Fight for Control of Central Bank Threatens Libya’s Uneasy Peace
2024-08-21.abstract: The country has settled into a tense east-west divide, but turmoil around the central bank, which handles Libya’s oil riches, has worsened a fraying, fragile political setup.

2024-08-21.title: They Spouted Hate Online. Then They Were Arrested.
2024-08-21.abstract: As hundreds of people appear in court for their role in recent anti-immigrant riots in Britain, several are accused of fueling disorder through online posts, raising questions about the limits of free speech.

2024-08-21.title: Russia Seeks to Turn Humbling Incursion Into Military Gains
2024-08-21.abstract: Two weeks after Ukraine launched its surprise attack, Russian forces have recovered from the initial shock and are trying to use the expanding war to their battlefield advantage.

2024-08-21.title: Thailand Reports Mpox Case in Traveler Who Arrived From Africa
2024-08-21.abstract: Officials are testing if the patient has the new version of the virus. If confirmed, it would be Thailand’s first case involving Clade Ib mpox.

2024-08-22.title: The War in Gaza Is Making Thousands of Orphans
2024-08-22.abstract: Extended families, hospital staff and volunteers are stepping in to care for Gaza’s many newly orphaned children, some of whom are injured, traumatized and haunted by memories of their parents.

2024-08-22.title: A Combat Medic’s Quest to Change Tributes to Fallen Soldiers
2024-08-22.abstract: Iryna Tsybukh, who was killed on the front line in Ukraine, wanted to humanize the way Kyiv commemorated the war dead. She also left notes for her own funeral.

2024-08-22.title: Canadian Government Orders Arbitration and End to Rail Freight Shutdown
2024-08-22.abstract: The shutdown had threatened to disrupt trade with the United States and other countries, as well as cause serious economic consequences in Canada.

2024-08-22.title: Mike Lynch’s Body Is Found After Sicily Yacht Sinking, Official Says
2024-08-22.abstract: The authorities have recovered the bodies of all but one of the six people missing after disaster struck a yacht carrying the British software tycoon.

2024-08-22.title: Sugar Industry Faces Pressure Over Coerced Hysterectomies and Labor Abuses
2024-08-22.abstract: After an investigation that ran in The New York Times, the industry is edging toward change. But companies in western India are reluctant to abandon an abusive labor system.

2024-08-23.title: A History Museum Shows How China Wants to Remake Hong Kong
2024-08-23.abstract: A new exhibit calls for the city’s residents to be patriotic, loyal to the Chinese Communist Party and ever vigilant to supposed threats to the state.

2024-08-23.title: Families of Dead Hostages Vent Anger at Israeli Leaders Over War
2024-08-23.abstract: After Israel recovered the bodies of six men who died in Hamas captivity in Gaza, survivors accused the government of sinking the chances of hostages returning alive through a cease-fire deal.

2024-08-23.title: South Korea Battery Plant Rushed Production Before Deadly Fire, Police Say
2024-08-23.abstract: The fire, one of the country’s worst industrial disasters in recent years, happened as the factory was rushing to fulfill a military contract, the authorities said.

2024-08-23.title: Sydney Metro Expands, Opening to Celebrity-Level Fanfare
2024-08-23.abstract: In a mostly car-dependent country where public transportation in major cities can be best described as “decent,” the expansion felt momentous.

2024-08-23.title: How to Be Truly Free: Lessons From a Philosopher President
2024-08-23.abstract: Pepe Mujica, Uruguay’s spartan former president and plain-spoken philosopher, offers wisdom from a rich life as he battles cancer.

2024-08-24.title: For Young Cambodians, a Mobile History Lesson From a Dark Time
2024-08-24.abstract: Most people in Cambodia are under 30, born long after the horrific rule of the Khmer Rouge. A bus is touring the country to make sure it’s not forgotten.

2024-08-24.title: The Loch Ness Monster Has Company in the Neighborhood: Wild Boars
2024-08-24.abstract: Local residents say the animals are roaming the roads and hills around the lake, tearing up lawns and terrorizing sheep.

2024-08-24.title: In a Grim Palestinian Refugee Community, People See Hope in Hamas
2024-08-24.abstract: In Ein al-Hilweh, Lebanon, long mired in poverty and despair, Hamas recruitment is up and the downtrodden air has given way to defiance and celebration.

2024-08-24.title: Mexico’s President Bet Big on Oil. His Successor Will Be Stuck With the Tab.
2024-08-24.abstract: Mexico’s next leader, Claudia Sheinbaum, is a climate scientist who has signaled a clean energy pivot. But a huge wager on fossil fuels by her political mentor stands in her way.

2024-08-24.title: Not Only for Killing: Drones Are Now Detecting Land Mines in Ukraine
2024-08-24.abstract: Ukraine is a beta test for embedding artificial intelligence and other new technologies in drones and robots to find deadly land mines, saving lives and allowing military forces to advance more quickly.

2024-08-25.title: The Young Frenchman Striving to Give Voice to a Troubled Area
2024-08-25.abstract: As a teenager, Amine Kessaci confronted Emmanuel Macron, founded an environmental group and lost a brother to drug violence. At 20, he almost won a National Assembly seat.

2024-08-25.title: Questions for Investigators Trying to Unravel Mystery of Luxury Yacht’s Sinking
2024-08-25.abstract: The investigators searching for answers about the shipwreck, leaving seven dead, face questions about extreme weather and possible human error or problems with the yacht itself.

2024-08-25.title: Trying to Head Off War, U.S. Moves Naval Forces Closer to Israel
2024-08-25.abstract: American officials are sending a signal to Iran and its allies, in the form of two aircraft carrier groups and a guided-missile submarine.

2024-08-25.title: Here’s a Timeline of Recent Strikes and Retaliations in the Middle East
2024-08-25.abstract: Tensions in the region had been high for weeks before Israel and Hezbollah began more intense cross-border attacks early Sunday.

2024-08-25.title: What if China Invades? For Taiwan, a TV Show Raises Tough Questions.
2024-08-25.abstract: Some think the drama, “Zero Day,” helps Taiwan confront an increasingly plausible scenario. Others say the show is alarmist and a tool of the government.

2024-08-26.title: 1 Tourist Is Dead and 1 Injured After Ice Canyon Collapses in Iceland
2024-08-26.abstract: Rescuers on Monday called off a search for what they initially believed were two other trapped tourists after it became clear that all the visitors were accounted for.

2024-08-26.title: Fox Hunters in the U.K. Want Protected Status Under Discrimination Law
2024-08-26.abstract: A lobbying group is preparing a bid to define hunting with animals as a protected belief. Many experts have questions.

2024-08-26.title: Israel Averts All-Out War With Hezbollah, but Its Regional Conflicts Grind On
2024-08-26.abstract: Relief that an eruption of violence across the border between Israel and Lebanon has been contained is tempered by a lack of progress in ending the fighting in Gaza.

2024-08-26.title: Gaza Cease-Fire Talks Will Continue in Cairo, Officials Say
2024-08-26.abstract: Four days of high-level meetings failed to achieve a breakthrough, but people briefed on the talks said discussions on technical issues would take place in the coming days.

2024-08-26.title: A Fire in East London Recalls Deadly Grenfell Cladding Scandal
2024-08-26.abstract: More than 80 people were evacuated in the early hours of Monday from a building that had used similar insulation.

2024-08-27.title: Prosecutor Turned Politician: Could Britain’s Leader Show Harris a Path to Power?
2024-08-27.abstract: Like Kamala Harris, Prime Minister Keir Starmer was a prosecutor before embracing politics. The two share a few other traits.

2024-08-27.title: In Eastern Ukraine, Terrifying Bombardment and Near Total Destruction
2024-08-27.abstract: Powerful guided “glide bombs” have helped Russia raze entire towns with ever greater speed. “When you drive into a ruined town, it’s like hopelessness,” one soldier said.

2024-08-27.title: A Mercedes-Benz Fire Jolts South Korea’s E.V. Transition
2024-08-27.abstract: The government is aggressively promoting electric cars. But a dramatic blaze in an underground lot has left some potential buyers worried about safety.

2024-08-27.title: Typhoon Shanshan Approaches Japan, Bringing Heavy Rain and Winds
2024-08-27.abstract: Japan’s southern islands were forecast to receive the most rain. The powerful storm has forced flight cancellations and disrupted high-speed rail travel.

2024-08-27.title: Russia Launches Deadly New Wave of Missiles and Drones at Ukraine
2024-08-27.abstract: The bombardment that began on Monday could have additional significance, coming three weeks after the Ukrainian military’s incursion into Russia’s Kursk region.

2024-08-28.title: With Hezbollah-Israel Conflict Contained, Iran’s Next Move May Be Modest
2024-08-28.abstract: After weeks of regional fears about a broader war, Hezbollah’s limited attack on Israel suggests that Iran, like its ally, wants to hem in the risk of escalation.

2024-08-28.title: In Quebec’s Casse-Croûtes, Fast Food for a Short but Sweet Summer
2024-08-28.abstract: By now, the most famous product of Quebec’s casse-croûtes, the poutine, has gone international. But can the guédille or the pinso ever become just as popular?

2024-08-28.title: Why Hasn’t Russia Kicked Out Ukrainian Invaders? Its Own Invaders Are Busy.
2024-08-28.abstract: Despite its overwhelming firepower, Russia has been unable for weeks to push Ukrainian troops back across the border, with the failure as much the result of priorities as a lack of personnel.

2024-08-28.title: Typhoon Shanshan Barrels Into Japan, Triggering Rare Warnings
2024-08-28.abstract: The authorities said the storm could produce a large-scale disaster as it approached the mainland. Nearly 1 million people were told to evacuate, and Toyota closed all its factories on Wednesday afternoon.

2024-08-28.title: The East Rises in Germany, and So Does Political Extremism
2024-08-28.abstract: Closely watched elections in the former East Germany are likely to reveal a still-divided country and yield a worrying challenge to Berlin.

2024-08-29.title: Tropical Storm Shanshan Drenches Southern Japan, Disrupting Train and Air Travel
2024-08-29.abstract: The storm weakened after making landfall as a typhoon, still posed the risk of landslides and floods as it moved at a crawling speed.

2024-08-29.title: Hong Kong Editors Convicted of Sedition in Blow to Press Freedom
2024-08-29.abstract: The editors said they published stories in the public interest. A judge ruled they were guilty of a crime against national security.

2024-08-29.title: Inquiry Into ‘Killer Nurse’ Won’t Weigh Key Question: Is She Innocent?
2024-08-29.abstract: More than 20 experts have called on the U.K. government to delay a public inquiry into the case of Lucy Letby, who was convicted of murdering babies, because of growing concerns about the evidence.

2024-08-29.title: The Woman Shaking Up German Politics From the East
2024-08-29.abstract: Sahra Wagenknecht, a former Communist, has founded her own party to respond to German grievances about migrants, crime and the dangers of the war in Ukraine.

2024-08-29.title: Israel’s Hostage Rescue Highlights Challenge of Hamas Tunnels in Gaza
2024-08-29.abstract: Gaza’s vast and complex subterranean network shelters many of Hamas’s remaining leaders, and is a key obstacle to eradicating the group.

2024-08-30.title: Kiingi Tuheitia, King of the Maori in New Zealand, Dies at 69
2024-08-30.abstract: The king, who had held the title for 18 years, was praised by New Zealand’s prime minister for his “unwavering commitment to his people.”

2024-08-30.title: Tropical Depression Shanshan Soaks Japan, Raising Flood and Landslide Risks
2024-08-30.abstract: The weakened storm still packed heavy rain and strong winds. It has killed four people, injured over 80 and prompted evacuation orders for millions.

2024-08-30.title: Preaching the Mormon Gospel in Remotest Australia, With Art
2024-08-30.abstract: In Northern Territory communities, missionaries rely on a special Indigenous edition of the Book of Mormon to get their message out.

2024-08-30.title: Parts of Italy’s Appian Way Are Left Off World Heritage List. Cue Grievances.
2024-08-30.abstract: For the towns along the ancient road that were not included on the UNESCO list, the slight is about more than hurt pride.

2024-08-30.title: Paris Is Utopia for Paralympians Until They Leave the Athletes’ Village
2024-08-30.abstract: The city built highly accessible accommodations for competitors in the 2024 Paralympic Games. But the rest of Paris remains difficult to navigate.

2024-08-31.title: A New Statue of a British Colonialist Exposes a Divide in Singapore
2024-08-31.abstract: The tribute to Sir Thomas Stamford Raffles, who is considered the founder of Singapore, has raised questions about how the wealthy city-state perceives its history.

2024-08-31.title: The Angry East
2024-08-31.abstract: As east Germans vote, a region in decline is searching for answers on the political extremes.

2024-08-31.title: What We Know About the Polio Crisis in Gaza
2024-08-31.abstract: Israel and Hamas have agreed to brief pauses in fighting so that humanitarian workers can begin an inoculation campaign.

2024-08-31.title: Hurling in Ireland: Is the ‘Clash of the Ash’ Becoming a ‘Battle of Bamboo’?
2024-08-31.abstract: For centuries, the sport’s wooden sticks have been made from Ireland’s ash trees. But with a disease destroying forests, the ancient game is turning to different materials.

2024-08-31.title: Russia’s Youngest Conscripts Unexpectedly See Combat Against Ukraine’s Invasion
2024-08-31.abstract: The long-sacrosanct practice of keeping young Russian army conscripts off the front lines is eroding as the lack of troops in Russia’s Kursk region indicates a manpower shortage.
